verse_specific_anchors: dehī nityam avadhyaḥ — the embodied is eternally inviolable
dehe sarvasya — in the body of all (universal scope, not just named individuals)
tasmāt — therefore (the closing pivot specific to 2.30)
sarvāṇi bhūtāni — all beings (the universalising shift)
the bridge function: 2.30 closes the imperishable, 2.31 opens duty
verdict_reason: The engineering analog anchors specifically on the closing 'tasmāt' and the pivot from doctrinal argument to duty. The phrasing 'therefore — and the therefore is the whole point of this verse — do not grieve any particular system's eventual decommissioning' directly mirrors the rhetorical structure of dehī nityam avadhyaḥ ... tasmāt ... na tvaṁ śocitum arhasi. This bridge function is unique to 2.30 — no other verse in the block ends with 'now act.'
